learning_outcomes.description: Understand and apply the concept of intellectual property and the need for its protection and management, including commercialisation.
Analyse and evaluate the need to build a strategy for protecting and exploiting IP, which leads to a commercially or non-commercially focussed outcome.
Apply, analyse and evaluate the basic principles and alternative steps of taking commercial and non-commercial research to their audience.
Create paths to commercialisation based upon decision making regarding IP profiles, portfolios and business objectives.

Communicate the strategic paths to commercialisation based upon decision making regarding IP profiles, portfolios and business objectives, including alternative steps of taking commercial and non-commercial research to their audience.

assessments.task_description:
In this piece of assessment you will introduce and propose a potential biotechnology product, process or service that you will focus on in your Commercialisation Feasibility Analysis and Presentation, and what value it could provide to society. Detailed instructions on how to complete this task are provided on Blackboard.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing the assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

However please note that this this task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may appropriately use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your Product Proposal through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
In this piece of assessment you will submit a draft written report for feedback. Your Commercialisation Feasibility Analysis Presentation is a complete evaluation of the commercialisation feasibility of a potential biotechnology product, process, or service that could emanate from your honours research. You will present your full analysis in week 12. Prior to this presentation, you will submit a draft written report outlining your findings for each step and summarising your research for each step, for the course coordinator to provide feedback prior to your presentation. Dot points are acceptable, and tables and figures are highly encouraged. Detailed instructions on how to complete this task are provided on Blackboard.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing the assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

However please note that this this task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may appropriately use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your draft report through the course submission point in Turnitin. Before submitting your assessment item to please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Presentation slides due through Blackboard 2pm 19/10/2026

Presentation time to be determined via sign-up sheet on blackboard. Presentations will take place 19/10/2026 - 23/10/2026
assessments.task_description:
Presenting is an important part of research commercialisation. In this assessment item students will present their full Commercialisation Feasibility Analysis following the 12-step framework taught in class. Students must submit their slides to the blackboard submission link by 2pm on Monday 19th October, and changes cannot be made to slides after submission. Students will then select a time to present during the week of the 19th October, via a Blackboard sign-up sheet, which will be released prior to the week of the 19th of October. Students are expected to attend the entire presentation slot they select (2 hour duration). Detailed instructions on how to complete this task are provided on Blackboard.

All presentations will be recorded.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your Commercialisation Feasibility Assessment Presentation slides through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
